mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods where FEDFUNDS declines, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive month-over-month growth in real sales of final goods and housing starts confirms broad-based demand expansion, supporting higher corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods where both series expand simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where industrial production falls below its 12-month trend during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating inflationary pressures, prompting immediate risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods following inflation spikes above the 0.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods where payrolls rise and unemployment falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic contraction, reducing aggregate corporate revenues and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods following negative GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in industrial production exceeding 0.8% signals resolving supply chain constraints and improving manufacturing efficiency, boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of triggered instances despite the production acceleration.
